solve_notes: Added two new `isCloseTo` overloads to `AbstractDateAssert`: `isCloseTo(Date, long, TimeUnit)` and `isCloseTo(String, long, TimeUnit)`. Both validate that `delta >= 0` using `checkArgument`, convert the delta to milliseconds with `unit.toMillis(delta)`, and delegate to the existing `Dates.assertIsCloseTo`. Also added the missing `static import` for `checkArgument` from `Preconditions`.
task_text:
Add TimeUnit-based isCloseTo overloads to AbstractDateAssert

In `assertj-core/src/main/java/org/assertj/core/api/AbstractDateAssert.java`,
`isCloseTo` only accepts a delta in raw milliseconds via
`isCloseTo(Date other, long deltaInMilliseconds)`. There is no overload
accepting a `java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit`, forcing callers to convert
manually (e.g., `TimeUnit.SECONDS.toMillis(5)`). Add
`isCloseTo(Date other, long delta, TimeUnit unit)` and
`isCloseTo(String dateAsString, long delta, TimeUnit unit)` to
`AbstractDateAssert` that compute `unit.toMillis(delta)` and delegate to
`Dates.assertIsCloseTo` in `internal/Dates.java`. Validate that `delta`
is non-negative in the new overloads, throwing `IllegalArgumentException`
with a descriptive message when it is not.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: exercises isCloseTo(Date, long) which is the delegation target of the new overload; covers assertIsCloseTo call
exercises isCloseTo(String, long) which is the delegation target of the new String overload
